creator: transitive@gmail.com
text:
Hopefully, I have followed the instructions properly. I still get the bad file descriptor in the log of the build. I also have noticed that another port on this system (java/openjdk8) for which I submitted a bug here https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=217846 has a build issue related to a bad file descriptor and this may or may not be related.

>>Also make sure, that you don't hit limits kern.maxfiles or kern.maxfilesperproc

How do I see whether kern.maxfiles or kern.maxfilesperproc have hit their limits? This is a new installation of freebsd on this system so I haven't done any tuning to any sysctl's. I am following 11-stable.

Here's the system details. 

FreeBSD alto.localdomain 11.0-STABLE FreeBSD 11.0-STABLE #0 r315336: Wed Mar 15 20:06:21 EDT 2017     root@alto.localdomain:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/GENERIC  amd64

As I just set this FreeBSD system up the other day, I only use Synth to manage ports.  However, I have used poudriere before briefly. I can set it up but it will take some time since poudriere is a bit more involved to set up. I have to read up in the handbook about how to use poudriere testport as well.
